key_points: Light travels in straight lines, which allows shadows to form
Shadows depend on the position of the light source, object and screen
Opaque objects create clear shadows, while translucent objects form faint shadows
Reflection of light occurs when light strikes a surface and bounces back
Mirrors are smooth surfaces that reflect light clearly
examples: A shadow becomes longer in the evening due to the position of the Sun
Frosted glass is a translucent material that allows partial light
A plane mirror forms a clear reflection of objects
Hand shadow puppets formed on a wall using a torch
formulas: Law of Reflection → Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection
Shadow formation → Light source + Object + Screen
exam_tips: Understand factors affecting the size of shadows
Draw diagrams showing reflection in a plane mirror
Differentiate between transparent, translucent and opaque objects
